2603.24213.title: Uncovering Memorization in Timeseries Imputation models: LBRM Membership Inference and its link to attribute Leakage
2603.24213.paper_summary: Deep learning models for time series imputation are now essential in fields such as healthcare, the Internet of Things (IoT), and finance. However, their deployment raises critical privacy concerns. Beyond the well-known issue of unintended memorization, which has been extensively studied in generative models, we demonstrate that time series models are vulnerable to inference attacks in a black-box setting. In this work, we introduce a two-stage attack framework comprising: (1) a novel membership inference attack based on a reference model that improves detection accuracy, even for models robust to overfitting-based attacks, and (2) the first attribute inference attack that predicts sensitive characteristics of the training data for timeseries imputation model. We evaluate these attacks on attention-based and autoencoder architectures in two scenarios: models that are trained from scratch, and fine-tuned models where the adversary has access to the initial weights. Our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed membership attack retrieves a significant portion of the training data with a tpr@top25% score significantly higher than a naive attack baseline. We show that our membership attack also provides a good insight of whether attribute inference will work (with a precision of 90% instead of 78% in the genral case).
2603.24213.paper_summary_zh: 深度學習模型在時間序列填補中現在已成為醫療保健、物聯網 (IoT) 和金融等領域的必要工具。<lb/>然而，它們的部署引發了關鍵的隱私問題。<lb/>除了在生成模型中廣泛研究的意外記憶問題外，我們展示了時間序列模型在黑箱環境中易受推斷攻擊的脆弱性。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們介紹了一個兩階段的攻擊框架，包括：(1) 一種基於參考模型的新穎會員推斷攻擊，該模型提高了檢測準確性，即使對於抗過擬合攻擊的模型也是如此，(2) 第一個屬性推斷攻擊，預測時間序列填補模型訓練數據的敏感特徵。<lb/>我們在兩個場景中評估這些攻擊，分別是：從頭開始訓練的模型，以及對手可以訪問初始權重的微調模型。<lb/>我們的實驗結果顯示，所提出的會員攻擊能夠檢索到大量的訓練數據，其 tpr@top25% 分數顯著高於天真的攻擊基線。<lb/>我們還表明，我們的會員攻擊對於屬性推斷是否有效提供了良好的見解（精確度為90%，而一般情況下為78%）。
2603.24213.author: Faiz Taleb et.al.
2603.24213.authors: Faiz Taleb, Ivan Gazeau, Maryline Laurent
